content:
- Read previous progress, task summary, done summary, and the list-active-tasks skill.
- Active backlog currently shows three failing Story 35 tasks; no failing bugs are visible in the summary.
- Next I am verifying blockers/priority in the task files and checking the actual repo stack/rules before selecting the next task.

- Confirmed Story 35 task files have no explicit <blocked_by> or <priority> tags.
- The repo stack is mixed Rust/Go/Nix, and the verify-service implementation currently lives in Go under cockroachdb_molt/molt/verifyservice with docs/openapi alongside it.
- Next I am refreshing the task summary and checking current verify-service config and HTTP surfaces to confirm which failing task is the right foundation to select.

- Refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md`; active backlog still contains exactly three failing Story 35 tasks and no active failing bugs.
- Inspected the real verify-service code: config.go still uses single-pair `verify.source.url` and `verify.destination.url`, while HTTP tests/service shape are still built around a single-job/single-pair flow.
- Task 1 is the correct next pick because Task 3 explicitly builds on it and Task 2 redesigns the HTTP UX around the new multi-database model that Task 1 introduces.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/01-task-verify-multi-db-config.md`.
- Reason: no active failing bugs; all three active tasks are failing non-bug tasks in Story 35; Task 1 is the foundational multi-db config change that unblocks the HTTP UX redesign and the credential-indirection follow-up.
- Next agent should start Task 1 with the tdd skill, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` via nix-backed make targets.

- Read prior Ralph progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed the active task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/01-task-verify-multi-db-config.md`.
- Next I am reading the task file and story task directory to determine whether this turn is planning-only (`TO BE VERIFIED` / blank) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).

- Read the current task file and confirmed it has no execution marker, so this turn must stop after writing a plan.
- Inspected the current verify-service config and runner code: it is still single-pair, operator-facing `url` based, and raw-table selection is only `source|destination` without any configured database selector.
- Next I am reading existing tests and a representative Ralph plan so I can write a concrete TDD execution sequence and identify the main boundary cleanup to flatten.

- Read the verify-service test surface and HTTP/service types to lock down the public contract before planning.
- Current public API still has no database selector on verify jobs, and raw-table requests only distinguish `source|destination`; both need explicit handling once multiple configured database mappings exist.
- Next I am doing one final pass over `RunRequest` / `JobRequest` before writing the plan file and wiring its path into the task markdown.

- Read the active Story 35 Task 1 file, confirmed it had no execution marker, and treated this turn as planning-only.
- Inspected the current verify-service config, runner, raw-table, HTTP, and command-test surfaces. Identified the main boundary issue: single-pair URL DTOs are mixed with runtime assumptions, and raw-table `database` actually means source/destination side.
- Wrote the plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-35-verify-multi-database-config/01-task-verify-multi-db-config_plans/2026-04-29-verify-multi-db-config-plan.md`, added its path to the task file, and ended the plan with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- The plan keeps Task 1 scoped to the structured multi-database config foundation plus the minimum selector needed to run one configured database, while explicitly deferring the broader HTTP UX redesign to Task 2.
- Next turn should start executing the plan via TDD vertical slices: config resolver first, then runner/raw-table selection, then CLI/docs, followed by `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Read previous Ralph progress, current task pointer, the task markdown, and the active plan.
- Confirmed the task is in execution mode because the plan ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the current verify-service code plus relevant improve-code-boundaries smell notes, then I will start the first TDD RED slice.

- Added the first RED slice: a structured multi-database config fixture plus a config test that resolves named database mappings.
- Ran the slice with `nix develop -c go test ./verifyservice -run TestLoadConfigSupportsStructuredMultiDatabaseDefaults` from `cockroachdb_molt/molt`; it failed as expected because `VerifyConfig.ResolveDatabase` does not exist yet.
- Next I am implementing the new config boundary: structured connection input types, a named-database resolver, and centralized connection-string building.

- Replaced the old operator-facing URL config with structured source/destination defaults plus per-database mappings and a resolved connection-pair boundary.
- Updated the runner to resolve one database selection before connecting, updated the CLI success summary to use resolved inventory, and adjusted package tests off the deleted URL DTO shape.
- Verified the first TDD slice is GREEN with `nix develop -c go test ./verifyservice -run TestLoadConfigSupportsStructuredMultiDatabaseDefaults`.
- Next I am adding the override-inheritance and no-default per-database config tests, then filling gaps exposed by those slices.

- Added and passed TDD slices for defaults-only multi-db config, per-database credential overrides, no-default fully specified per-database connections, duplicate-name rejection, missing inherited fields, invalid merged TLS, unsupported structured fields, and scalar database-entry rejection.
- Implemented parser-owned `verify.databases` decoding so scalar entries fail with a clear indexed path instead of a generic YAML type error.
- Next I am running the full verifyservice and cmd/verifyservice package tests to surface every remaining place still pinned to the removed single-URL contract.

- Updated verify-service config loading, runner selection, raw-table request shape, CLI validation output, tests, fixtures, docs, and OpenAPI for structured multi-database config.
- Required repo gates are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all exited successfully.
- Next I am marking the task file complete, switching Ralph tasks, then committing and pushing all changes as instructed.
